Some extensions merit more investigation across architectural, standards integration, and usability dimensions.

Context hierarchies would enable inheritance chains beyond base-to-context, allowing professional sub-contexts sharing common credentials. A physician could maintain a general "healthcare" context with sub-contexts for different institutions, each inheriting common credentials while overriding institution-specific contact information. This extension requires careful consideration of inheritance conflict resolution when multiple parent contexts define the same field.

Temporal versioning would create audit trails enabling queries such as "what was this user's professional identity on March 15, 2024?" The current schema includes temporal validity fields but does not implement versioned history. Full temporal support would enable compliance scenarios requiring historical record reconstruction while supporting legitimate identity evolution over time.

Integration with verifiable credentials (W3C VC Data Model) could enable selective disclosure of verified attributes without revealing full identity. A user could prove they are over 18 without revealing their birthdate, or prove professional credentials without revealing their employer. This integration would position the system within the emerging decentralized identity ecosystem while maintaining the usability advantages of centralized account management.

Cross-context consistency validation represents an important privacy enhancement. The system could warn users when information shared across contexts with different privacy levels might enable re-identification. For example, sharing the same profile photo across professional and pseudonymous contexts creates linkability that undermines the pseudonymous context's privacy guarantees.

The consent management system could evolve toward automated policy enforcement, where users define rules that the system enforces without per-request consent screens. A policy such as "never share my phone number with social applications" would automatically reject scope requests violating the rule, reducing consent fatigue while maintaining user control.

Formal usability studies with diverse user populations would validate whether the multi-context model improves user experience or introduces complexity that undermines adoption. The preliminary user research suggests demand for context separation, but systematic evaluation across cultural contexts and technical literacy levels remains necessary.

\newpage

# Appendix A: Code Availability {-}

The complete source code for this project is available at:

**Repository**: [https://github.com/mastrolinux/final-project](https://github.com/mastrolinux/final-project)

Access is available upon request to: **lc193@student.london.ac.uk**

The repository includes:

- Backend API implementation (Python/FastAPI)
- Frontend application (Vue 3/TypeScript)
- Database migrations (PostgreSQL/Supabase)
- Test suite (pytest)
- API documentation (OpenAPI/Swagger)
- Architecture documentation (Quarto)
- Postman collection for API testing

\newpage

# Appendix B: Data Model {-}

## Entity Relationship Diagram

@fig-datamodel presents the domain model as an entity-relationship diagram showing the core aggregates and their relationships. BaseProfile serves as the root aggregate with a one-to-one relationship to AuthUsers for credentials and one-to-many relationships to IdentityNames and ContextProfiles. The OAuth 2.1 subsystem comprises OAuthClients, OAuthConsents, and the token lifecycle tables (authorization codes, access tokens, refresh tokens). ContextProfiles optionally bind to OAuth tokens and consents, enabling context-aware third-party access.

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-datamodel
%%| fig-cap: "Entity-relationship diagram showing domain model"
%%| fig-width: 6.5
erDiagram
    BaseProfile ||--|| AuthUsers : authenticates
    BaseProfile ||--o{ IdentityNames : has
    BaseProfile ||--o{ ContextProfiles : has
    BaseProfile ||--o{ OAuthConsents : grants
    BaseProfile ||--o{ OAuthAccessTokens : owns
    OAuthClients ||--o{ OAuthConsents : receives
    OAuthClients ||--o{ OAuthAuthCodes : issues
    OAuthClients ||--o{ OAuthAccessTokens : issues
    OAuthAccessTokens ||--o| OAuthRefreshTokens : rotates
    ContextProfiles ||--o{ OAuthConsents : binds
    ContextProfiles ||--o{ OAuthAccessTokens : scopes

    BaseProfile {
        uuid user_id PK
        enum account_type
        string primary_email
        string preferred_language
        timestamp valid_from
        timestamp valid_to
        timestamp deleted_at
    }

    AuthUsers {
        uuid id PK
        uuid user_id FK
        string email
        string password_hash
        boolean is_email_verified
        integer failed_login_attempts
        timestamp locked_until
        boolean is_admin
    }

    IdentityNames {
        uuid id PK
        uuid identity_id FK
        enum name_type
        jsonb name_value
        boolean is_deprecated
        enum visibility_level
        timestamp valid_from
    }

    ContextProfiles {
        uuid id PK
        uuid user_id FK
        enum context_type
        string context_name
        string display_name_override
        string email_override
        string phone_override
        text bio
        timestamp valid_from
        timestamp valid_to
    }

    OAuthClients {
        string client_id PK
        string client_name
        array redirect_uris
        array allowed_scopes
        boolean is_confidential
        boolean is_first_party
        enum token_endpoint_auth_method
    }

    OAuthAuthCodes {
        string code PK
        string client_id FK
        uuid user_id FK
        string code_challenge
        uuid context_profile_id FK
        timestamp expires_at
        timestamp used_at
    }

    OAuthAccessTokens {
        uuid id PK
        string token_hash
        string client_id FK
        uuid user_id FK
        string scope
        uuid context_profile_id FK
        timestamp expires_at
        timestamp revoked_at
    }

    OAuthRefreshTokens {
        uuid id PK
        string token_hash
        uuid access_token_id FK
        string client_id FK
        uuid user_id FK
        timestamp expires_at
        timestamp rotated_at
        uuid replaced_by_id FK
    }

    OAuthConsents {
        uuid id PK
        uuid user_id FK
        string client_id FK
        array granted_scopes
        uuid context_profile_id FK
        timestamp granted_at
        timestamp withdrawn_at
        enum consent_method
    }
```

## JSONB Data Examples

The system uses PostgreSQL JSONB fields to support flexible, culturally-sensitive data structures. Key examples demonstrate the architecture's capacity to handle diverse naming conventions without imposing Western structural assumptions.

**Chinese Family-First Ordering (IdentityNames.name_value):**

```json
{
  "family": {
    "zh": "李",
    "zh-Latn": "Li",
    "en": "Li"
  },
  "given": {
    "zh": "明",
    "zh-Latn": "Ming",
    "en": "Ming"
  },
  "display_order": "family_first",
  "full_name": {
    "zh": "李明",
    "en": "Li Ming"
  }
}
```

**Indonesian Mononym (IdentityNames.name_value):**

```json
{
  "full_name": {
    "id": "Sukarno",
    "en": "Sukarno"
  },
  "explanation": "Mononym (single name)"
}
```

**Arabic Multi-Component (IdentityNames.name_value):**

```json
{
  "ism": {
    "ar": "محمد",
    "ar-Latn": "Muhammad"
  },
  "kunya": {
    "ar": "أبو عبد الله",
    "ar-Latn": "Abu Abdullah"
  },
  "nasab": {
    "ar": "بن عبد الله",
    "ar-Latn": "ibn Abdullah"
  },
  "display_preference": "formal_full"
}
```

**Context Profile Overrides (ContextProfiles.overrides):**

```json
{
  "display_name": "Dr. Sarah Chen, MD, PhD",
  "email": "s.chen@hospital.org",
  "bio": "Board-certified psychiatrist",
  "credentials": ["MD", "PhD", "Board Certified"]
}
```

These flexible structures enable the system to accommodate global naming conventions including Spanish double surnames, Tamil patronymic initials, and Western given-family patterns, all stored within a single schema supporting hundreds of languages without structural assumptions.

\newpage

# Appendix C: Test Suite Output {-}

The following output captures the complete test run with per-module coverage analysis, executed via `docker compose exec api pytest --cov=src --cov-report=term-missing`.

```{=latex}
\begin{footnotesize}
\begin{verbatim}
============================== test session starts ==============================
platform linux -- Python 3.12.12, pytest-7.4.4, pluggy-1.6.0
rootdir: /app
plugins: anyio-4.12.0, cov-4.1.0, asyncio-0.23.3, Faker-22.0.0
asyncio: mode=Mode.STRICT
collected 378 items

tests/integration/test_admin_oauth_endpoints.py ...........           [  2%]
tests/integration/test_auth_endpoints.py .....................        [  8%]
tests/integration/test_auth_integration.py ......                     [ 10%]
tests/integration/test_context_endpoints.py ..........................[ 16%]
tests/integration/test_database_integration.py ..........             [ 19%]
tests/integration/test_endpoints.py ............                      [ 22%]
tests/integration/test_oauth_endpoints.py ......................      [ 28%]
tests/unit/test_admin_dependency.py .....                              [ 29%]
tests/unit/test_auth_service.py .....................................  [ 42%]
tests/unit/test_config.py ..........                                   [ 44%]
tests/unit/test_context_service.py ............................        [ 52%]
tests/unit/test_database.py ..........                                 [ 54%]
tests/unit/test_models.py .............                                [ 58%]
tests/unit/test_oauth_repository.py ..................................[ 67%]
tests/unit/test_oauth_service.py .....................................  [ 80%]
tests/unit/test_profile_repository.py .............................    [ 88%]
tests/unit/test_profile_service.py .....                               [ 89%]
tests/unit/test_redis_client.py ..................                      [ 94%]
tests/unit/test_schemas.py ......................                      [100%]

---------- coverage: platform linux, python 3.12.12-final-0 ----------
Name                                  Stmts   Miss  Cover  Missing
------------------------------------------------------------------
src/api/dependencies/auth.py             40     12    70%  54,65,73,99-105
src/api/v1/endpoints/admin_oauth.py      67     10    85%  74,234,244-246
src/api/v1/endpoints/auth.py             92     18    80%  126-134,143-145
src/api/v1/endpoints/contexts.py         94     12    87%  72,132,270-280
src/api/v1/endpoints/database.py         41      6    85%  80-81,126-127
src/api/v1/endpoints/oauth.py           212    105    50%  165,209-210,271
src/api/v1/endpoints/profiles.py         58     36    38%  29-30,45-54
src/api/v1/router.py                     17      0   100%
src/core/config.py                      109      8    93%  129,137,160,179
src/core/database.py                     84     22    74%  16-19,61,82-84
src/core/redis_client.py                 82      3    96%  87,236-237
src/core/security.py                     63      6    90%  217-218,233-236
src/core/types.py                        14      2    86%  42,46
src/main.py                              27      3    89%  84,86,89
src/models/auth.py                       55      5    91%  99,114,116,126
src/models/context.py                    42     12    71%  90,94,108-119
src/models/oauth.py                     245     26    89%  39,48,58,63,77
src/models/profile.py                   106     10    91%  35,43,53,69
src/repositories/auth_repository.py      87      8    91%  55-60,231
src/repositories/context_repository.py   47      6    87%  161,191,210-220
src/repositories/oauth_repository.py    233     36    85%  103,113,125-133
src/repositories/profile_repository.py   81      2    98%  286,314
src/schemas/auth.py                      97      5    95%  51,59,104,189
src/schemas/context.py                   79      6    92%  55,62,107-109
src/schemas/oauth.py                    261      6    98%  102-104,110-112
src/schemas/profile.py                   96      5    95%  48,88,208-210
src/services/auth_service.py            114      3    97%  192,262,381
src/services/context_service.py         132     10    92%  142,307,312
src/services/oauth_service.py           216      6    97%  73,80,217,352-357
src/services/profile_service.py          63     34    46%  55,108-116
src/tasks/email_tasks.py                 52     40    23%  41-51,70-153
------------------------------------------------------------------
TOTAL                                  3031    463    85%

============================== 378 passed in 5.96s ==============================
\end{verbatim}
\end{footnotesize}
```

# References {-}

::: {#refs}
:::
